metadata.name: pyth_price_feed_node
metadata.description: On-chain Pyth price-condition node for the strategy_runtime workflow ecosystem

instructions.name: check_price
docs: Keeper-submitted price reading. Latches `triggered = true` the first
time the condition is met; subsequent calls fail until `reset_trigger`.
V2 will replace the trusted reading with on-chain Pyth verification.

accounts.name: caller
docs: Anyone can submit a reading — the feed is keyed by (creator, feed_id).
V2 will replace the keeper-trusted price with an on-chain Pyth verify.

instructions.name: initialize_feed
docs: Initialize a per-(creator, feed_id) price-condition feed.
`condition`: 0 = above, 1 = below, 2 = equal (±EQUAL_TOLERANCE).
`exponent` is the Pyth exponent for `target_price` (typically -8).
`max_staleness_secs` rejects reports older than N seconds (0 = off).

instructions.name: reset_trigger
docs: Creator-only — clear the latched trigger so the feed can fire again.

errors.name: InvalidTargetPrice
errors.msg: Target price must be greater than zero

errors.name: InvalidReportedPrice
errors.msg: Reported price must be greater than zero

errors.name: AlreadyTriggered
errors.msg: Feed has already been triggered — reset before reuse

errors.name: UnauthorizedCaller
errors.msg: Caller is not the creator of this feed

errors.name: StalePrice
errors.msg: Reported price is stale (timestamp older than max_staleness_secs)

types.name: PythFeedState
docs: Per-(creator, feed_id) price-condition state.
`triggered` flips to true the first time `check_price` observes a value
satisfying the configured condition. While triggered, all subsequent
`check_price` calls fail until `reset_trigger` is invoked by the creator.
`feed_id` is the 32-byte Pyth feed identifier (e.g. SOL/USD). It is
stored both for PDA derivation and so callers can sanity-check that the
keeper-supplied price comes from the right oracle.
V1 follows the keeper-submits-price pattern (mirrors `risk_guard_node`).
V2 can wrap a `pyth-solana-receiver-sdk` price account read so the
program verifies the Pyth update itself, removing keeper trust.

fields.name: feed_id
docs: Pyth feed identifier (32-byte hex, e.g. SOL/USD feed).

fields.name: target_price
docs: Target price in raw price units (apply `exponent` to interpret).

fields.name: exponent
docs: Pyth exponent (negative — e.g. -8 means raw / 1e8 = human price).

fields.name: condition
docs: 0 = above, 1 = below, 2 = equal (±EQUAL_TOLERANCE).

fields.name: max_staleness_secs
docs: Max acceptable age (seconds) between Pyth publish_time and this slot.
0 = no staleness check (NOT recommended in production).

fields.name: last_price
docs: Last reported price in raw units.

fields.name: last_publish_time
docs: Pyth publish_time of `last_price` (unix seconds).

fields.name: triggered
docs: Latched: true once the condition has been hit at least once.

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump seed.

fields.name: _reserved
docs: Reserved for future fields without re-deriving accounts.
